rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the target implementation scope when bindings depend on implementation-level subcomponents or connections.
Preserve AADL structure by not introducing new components, ports, or properties outside the target implementation.
generic_rules: Place component-type contracts in type annexes; place implementation-specific bindings in implementation annexes.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax with `eq`, `const`, `assume`, `guarantee`, or `assign` declarations.
Logical expressions must use AGREE types: int, real, bool; avoid AADL property names as variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) directly in AGREE; they are not in scope unless exposed as ports or constants.
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes; only use directly visible features.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement strategy for model_analyst
